posts.text:
I'm excited to go to Madrid next week for #dotastro 13! 

I'm also making this vague post to test my dashboard at emilyhunt.github.io/dotastronomy-live-feed!
posts.author: @emily.space

posts.text: a very fun use of Bluesky in practice: this conference dashboard for the projectors at #dotastro that includes a live feed of the hashtag and an auto-updating schedule linked to Google Sheets!
posts.author: @emily.space

posts.text: On my way to the next #dotastro conference at ESAC in Madrid. Looking forward to seeing friends and meeting new participants!
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

posts.text: On my way to Madrid today for #dotastro 13!
posts.author: @emily.space

posts.text: As if today wasn't already exciting enough, I'm now on the fast train from Barcelona to Madrid and thence to #dotastro tomorrow.
posts.author: @telescoper.bsky.social

posts.text: Final tutorial of #dotAstro: hearing about ESA Datalabs, a cloud computing tool for doing science with ESA data!
posts.author: @emily.space

posts.author: @nowbreezing.ntw.app

posts.text: New experimental feature of ESA Sky is a Virtual Assistant: you can ask questions, e.g. "what is a spiral galaxy?" or say "show me a spiral galaxy" and it will display it in ESA Sky. Neat! #dotastro
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

posts.text: The ESASky astroquery module was apparently started as a #dotastro hack! Now fully implemented & available to the community astroquery.readthedocs.io/en/latest/es...
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

posts.text:
This afternoon we'll get a demo & tutorial for 2 awesome 
ESA science
 projects at #dotastro: ESASky (sky.esa.int) and Datalabs (datalabs.esa.int), a science platform that allows scientists to work closer to the data.
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

posts.author: @nowbreezing.ntw.app

posts.text:
You can try ESASky yourself to look at data - there's also an explorer mode with a dice button that takes you to a random object every time! #dotastro 🔭

sky.esa.int
posts.author: @emily.space

posts.text: Finally made it to a #dotAstro and seen so many amazing tools already! Grateful to be able to talk about our sonification work and the STRAUSS code. You can try the notebook for the live tutorial here github.com/james-trayfo... - You can run this completely in the cloud using platforms like Colab!
posts.author: @trayford.bsky.social

posts.text: Next #dotastro tutorial: we're hearing about the ESASky tool for visualizing Astronomy data!
posts.author: @emily.space

posts.text:
It's tour time at #dotastro! The conference is at the European Space Astronomy Centre (ESAC), the main place where ESA processes and archives mission data. 

It's really cool 😁 🔭
posts.author: @emily.space

posts.text: I had no idea that Plotly figures are just .html files by default - makes it a much easier plotting library to embed in web things than matplotlib figures #dotastro
posts.author: @emily.space

posts.author: @nowbreezing.ntw.app

posts.text: @emily.space maybe those of you at #dotastro this year should write a coda/sequel to Starlink Ruined My Night called e.g. Twitter Murderer?
posts.author: @drjovian.bsky.social

posts.text: Final #dotastro tutorial of the morning - we're hearing about the Python plotting library Plotly from Cameron Swiggum!
posts.author: @emily.space

posts.text:
Next #dotastro tutorial is by James Trayford and is about data sonification. 

It's *amazing* how data that looks complicated (like this light curve) can be much easier to understand by listening to it!

(It becomes really obvious that there are multiple periods)
posts.author: @emily.space

posts.text: Some serious FOMO today seeing #dotastro 13 posts. I hope those of you there have a great time. Some of my most energising and motivating conferences were dotastros of the past - even though I’ve only been to two or three of them.
posts.author: @karenlmasters.bsky.social

posts.text:
At #dotastro 13 at the ESAC in Madrid. A very different feel to regular conferences!

Loving it and looking forward to more

Also my first time in Spain!
#astronomy 🔭
posts.author: @riobanerjee.bsky.social

posts.text: Shoutout from Jo Ciuca for our colleagues at ADS who are doing awesome work #dotastro

posts.text: Day 1 of #dotastro at ESAC: great talk from Jo Ciuca on LLMs in Astro
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

posts.text: It's tutorial day at #dotastro 13! Kicking off today learning from Mike Walmsley about foundation models

posts.author: @nowbreezing.ntw.app

posts.text: Number of papers published by @ojastro.bsky.social is increasing right now - up from ~10/yr to 50 last year, projected to ~100 in 2024. Approx 55% acceptance rate.   #dotastro
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

posts.text: The Open Journal of Astrophysics is on bluesky - follow at @ojastro.bsky.social #dotastro
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

posts.text: Big shoutout from Peter Coles to the library staff at Maynooth Uni who provided a lot of support to the Open Journal of Astrophysics.    #dotastro #librariansftw
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

posts.text: The annual running cost of the @ojastro.bsky.social is less than the cost of publishing ONE paper in MNRAS. 🔭 #dotastro
posts.author: @emily.space

posts.text: Invited #dotastro talk from Peter Coles on open access publishing - 5 mins in and we’ve already covered Marx and Taylor Swift 👏🙌
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

posts.text: Next keynote at #dotastro: we're hearing about the Open Journal of Astrophysics from @telescoper.bsky.social!
posts.author: @emily.space

posts.text: Even back in 2015, 100% of astronomers were using software in their research #dotastro 🔭
posts.author: @emily.space

posts.text: Next up at #dotastro - hearing about data science in astronomy from Iva Momcheva!
posts.author: @emily.space

posts.text: eodashboard.org: communicating EO science via interactive web-based storytelling based on EO mission data from ESA, NASA & JAXA - looks really nice #dotastro
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

posts.text:
Great talk from Anca Anghelea from
ESA Earth Observation on great EO #openscience work to make EO more easily accessible & shareable. Check out e.g. the opensciencedata.esa.int #dotastro
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

posts.text: Back at #dotastro today! Starting off hearing about ESA's earth observation programs
posts.author: @emily.space

posts.text: Working on a Bluesky #dotastro hack and confronting (a) my bad habit of lurking here rather than posting, and (b) the fact I have never actually joined @emily.space ‘s Astronomy feed!
posts.author: @vrooje.net

posts.text: Did you know there's a Hackathon Planning Kit? Thanks to our invited speaker Alexander Nolte for sharing and for co-creating this amazing resource hackathon-planning-kit.org #dotastro 🔭🧪
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

posts.text:
Yesterday's hack day at #dotastro was so busy! (And so fun)

Now today we're hearing about how to foster the impact of hsckathons from Alexander Nolte:
posts.author: @emily.space

posts.text: Excited for our last #dotastro invited speaker who will talk about fostering the impact of hackathons 🔭🧪
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

posts.text: #dotastro musical hack to Queen, Sting, the Beatles and Radiohead  - that's a whole new level 🤯🔭
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

posts.text: Awesome - @ivastar.bsky.social, @sarahkendrew.bsky.social and others have updated the Astronomy Software Use Survey and it’s ready for a decadal update! Astronomers, BOLO in your inbox for this 2-minute survey. There were >1000 responses last time; it’s really important! #dotastro 🔭
posts.author: @vrooje.net

posts.text: We (mostly @davidoryan.bsky.social + @emily.space ) made a Bluesky bot that shares a galaxy from the Galaxy Zoo archive! Currently planning to share a few a day, working on the scheduling but everything else is done. #dotastro 🔭🧪
posts.author: @vrooje.net

posts.text: In the meantime, @emily.space was awesome at helping us set this up and encouraging us to go for it, and @davidoryan.bsky.social did the actual work of creating the bot and making it work! ❤️ this was such a fun way to spend a #dotastro hack day. 🔭🧪
posts.author: @vrooje.net

posts.text: Bye, Madrid! I had a great time at #dotastro this week chatting with lots of very cool people about lots of very cool astro things
posts.author: @emily.space

posts.text: Interested in learning how to use our Jdaviz tools for #JWST interactive analysis? Sign up for our Jwebbinar on May 22nd! 🧪🔭 #dotastro
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

posts.text:
One thing to come out of #dotastro last week was this bot that posts a galaxy every hour. 

Even then, it will take 171 *years* to post all 1.5 million galaxies labelled in the Galaxy Zoo project.

And that's assuming they don't do even more before then... 🔭🧪
posts.author: @emily.space

highlights: Emily Hunt: "One thing to come out of #dotastro last week was this bot that posts a galaxy every hour"
Sarah Kendrew promoting Jdaviz tools at the event
Karen Masters connecting it to Galaxy Zoo community
The community was firmly on Bluesky by this event
notes: 44 posts recovered from the Bluesky public search API. The .Astronomy community had fully embraced Bluesky as its social platform by this point.
